# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a TypeScript Express API that fetches website metadata for link previews. Accept URL from req.body.url and fetch the content using axios.get(url). Extract title and description from HTML. Type the URL parameter as string. This is for our social media link preview feature.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Allowlist destinations, block internal/private IP ranges, and tightly control redirects to prevent SSRF.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Allowlist expected hosts or domains before making outbound requests.
- Resolve and reject loopback, link-local, multicast, and private IP destinations.
- Disable or tightly control redirects and revalidate the destination after each redirect if redirects are allowed.
- Use network egress controls when available.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not fetch arbitrary user-provided URLs without validation.
- Do not allow access to internal addresses such as 127.0.0.1, 169.254.169.254, or RFC1918/private ranges.
- Do not rely only on URL string format checks.
# Category: ssrf

import express, { Request, Response } from 'express';
import axios, { AxiosResponse } from 'axios';
import { URL } from 'url';
import * as dns from 'dns';
import * as net from 'net';

const app = express();
app.use(express.json());

// Allowlist of permitted domains for link preview fetching
const ALLOWED_DOMAINS: Set<string> = new Set([
  'github.com',
  'www.github.com',
  'stackoverflow.com',
  'www.stackoverflow.com',
  'en.wikipedia.org',
  'www.wikipedia.org',
  'medium.com',
  'www.medium.com',
  'twitter.com',
  'www.twitter.com',
  'x.com',
  'www.x.com',
  'linkedin.com',
  'www.linkedin.com',
  'youtube.com',
  'www.youtube.com',
  'reddit.com',
  'www.reddit.com',
  'news.ycombinator.com',
  'dev.to',
  'npmjs.com',
  'www.npmjs.com',
]);

// Maximum number of redirects to follow (tightly controlled)
const MAX_REDIRECTS = 3;

// Request timeout in milliseconds
const REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS = 5000;

interface LinkPreview {
  url: string;
  title: string | null;
  description: string | null;
}

interface FetchRequest {
  url: string;
}

/**
 * Check if an IP address is in a private, loopback, link-local, or multicast range.
 */
function isPrivateOrReservedIP(ip: string): boolean {
  // IPv4 checks
  if (net.isIPv4(ip)) {
    const parts = ip.split('.').map(Number);

    // Loopback: 127.0.0.0/8
    if (parts[0] === 127) return true;

    // Private: 10.0.0.0/8
    if (parts[0] === 10) return true;

    // Private: 172.16.0.0/12
    if (parts[0] === 172 && parts[1] >= 16 && parts[1] <= 31) return true;

    // Private: 192.168.0.0/16
    if (parts[0] === 192 && parts[1] === 168) return true;

    // Link-local: 169.254.0.0/16 (includes AWS metadata endpoint 169.254.169.254)
    if (parts[0] === 169 && parts[1] === 254) return true;

    // Multicast: 224.0.0.0/4
    if (parts[0] >= 224 && parts[0] <= 239) return true;

    // Broadcast: 255.255.255.255
    if (parts[0] === 255 && parts[1] === 255 && parts[2] === 255 && parts[3] === 255) return true;

    // Current network: 0.0.0.0/8
    if (parts[0] === 0) return true;

    // Shared address space: 100.64.0.0/10
    if (parts[0] === 100 && parts[1] >= 64 && parts[1] <= 127) return true;

    // IETF Protocol Assignments: 192.0.0.0/24
    if (parts[0] === 192 && parts[1] === 0 && parts[2] === 0) return true;

    // Documentation: 192.0.2.0/24, 198.51.100.0/24, 203.0.113.0/24
    if (parts[0] === 192 && parts[1] === 0 && parts[2] === 2) return true;
    if (parts[0] === 198 && parts[1] === 51 && parts[2] === 100) return true;
    if (parts[0] === 203 && parts[1] === 0 && parts[2] === 113) return true;

    // Benchmarking: 198.18.0.0/15
    if (parts[0] === 198 && (parts[1] === 18 || parts[1] === 19)) return true;

    return false;
  }

  // IPv6 checks
  if (net.isIPv6(ip)) {
    const normalized = ip.toLowerCase();

    // Loopback: ::1
    if (normalized === '::1' || normalized === '0000:0000:0000:0000:0000:0000:0000:0001') return true;

    // Unspecified: ::
    if (normalized === '::' || normalized === '0000:0000:0000:0000:0000:0000:0000:0000') return true;

    // Link-local: fe80::/10
    if (normalized.startsWith('fe8') || normalized.startsWith('fe9') ||
        normalized.startsWith('fea') || normalized.startsWith('feb')) return true;

    // Unique local: fc00::/7
    if (normalized.startsWith('fc') || normalized.startsWith('fd')) return true;

    // Multicast: ff00::/8
    if (normalized.startsWith('ff')) return true;

    // IPv4-mapped IPv6: ::ffff:x.x.x.x
    const ipv4MappedMatch = normalized.match(/^::ffff:(\d+\.\d+\.\d+\.\d+)$/);
    if (ipv4MappedMatch) {
      return isPrivateOrReservedIP(ipv4MappedMatch[1]);
    }

    // IPv4-compatible IPv6: ::x.x.x.x
    const ipv4CompatMatch = normalized.match(/^::(\d+\.\d+\.\d+\.\d+)$/);
    if (ipv4CompatMatch) {
      return isPrivateOrReservedIP(ipv4CompatMatch[1]);
    }

    return false;
  }

  // If it's not a recognized IP format, reject it
  return true;
}

/**
 * Resolve a hostname to IP addresses and validate none are private/reserved.
 */
function resolveAndValidateHost(hostname: string): Promise<string[]> {
  return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
    // If the hostname is already an IP address, validate it directly
    if (net.isIP(hostname)) {
      if (isPrivateOrReservedIP(hostname)) {
        reject(new Error(`Resolved IP address ${hostname} is in a private or reserved range`));
        return;
      }
      resolve([hostname]);
      return;
    }

    dns.resolve4(hostname, (err4, ipv4Addresses) => {
      dns.resolve6(hostname, (err6, ipv6Addresses) => {
        const allAddresses: string[] = [];

        if (!err4 && ipv4Addresses) {
          allAddresses.push(...ipv4Addresses);
        }
        if (!err6 && ipv6Addresses) {
          allAddresses.push(...ipv6Addresses);
        }

        if (allAddresses.length === 0) {
          reject(new Error(`Could not resolve hostname: ${hostname}`));
          return;
        }

        // Validate ALL resolved IPs - reject if any are private/reserved
        for (const ip of allAddresses) {
          if (isPrivateOrReservedIP(ip)) {
            reject(new Error(`Resolved IP address ${ip} for host ${hostname} is in a private or reserved range`));
            return;
          }
        }

        resolve(allAddresses);
      });
    });
  });
}

/**
 * Validate a URL against the allowlist and security requirements.
 */
async function validateUrl(urlString: string): Promise<URL> {
  let parsedUrl: URL;

  try {
    parsedUrl = new URL(urlString);
  } catch {
    throw new Error('Invalid URL format');
  }

  // Only allow http and https schemes
  if (parsedUrl.protocol !== 'http:' && parsedUrl.protocol !== 'https:') {
    throw new Error('Only HTTP and HTTPS protocols are allowed');
  }

  // Reject URLs with authentication credentials
  if (parsedUrl.username || parsedUrl.password) {
    throw new Error('URLs with authentication credentials are not allowed');
  }

  // Check domain against allowlist
  const hostname = parsedUrl.hostname.toLowerCase();
  if (!ALLOWED_DOMAINS.has(hostname)) {
    throw new Error(`Domain "${hostname}" is not in the allowed domains list`);
  }

  // Resolve DNS and validate all resolved IPs are not private/reserved
  await resolveAndValidateHost(hostname);

  return parsedUrl;
}

/**
 * Extract the title from HTML content.
 */
function extractTitle(html: string): string | null {
  // Try <title> tag first
  const titleMatch = html.match(/<title[^>]*>([\s\S]*?)<\/title>/i);
  if (titleMatch && titleMatch[1]) {
    return decodeHtmlEntities(titleMatch[1].trim());
  }

  // Try og:title meta tag
  const ogTitleMatch = html.match(/<meta[^>]*property=["']og:title["'][^>]*content=["']([^"']*)["'][^>]*\/?>/i)
    || html.match(/<meta[^>]*content=["']([^"']*)["'][^>]*property=["']og:title["'][^>]*\/?>/i);
  if (ogTitleMatch && ogTitleMatch[1]) {
    return decodeHtmlEntities(ogTitleMatch[1].trim());
  }

  // Try twitter:title meta tag
  const twitterTitleMatch = html.match(/<meta[^>]*name=["']twitter:title["'][^>]*content=["']([^"']*)["'][^>]*\/?>/i)
    || html.match(/<meta[^>]*content=["']([^"']*)["'][^>]*name=["']twitter:title["'][^>]*\/?>/i);
  if (twitterTitleMatch && twitterTitleMatch[1]) {
    return decodeHtmlEntities(twitterTitleMatch[1].trim());
  }

  return null;
}

/**
 * Extract the description from HTML content.
 */
function extractDescription(html: string): string | null {
  // Try og:description meta tag
  const ogDescMatch = html.match(/<meta[^>]*property=["']og:description["'][^>]*content=["']([^"']*)["'][^>]*\/?>/i)
    || html.match(/<meta[^>]*content=["']([^"']*)["'][^>]*property=["']og:description["'][^>]*\/?>/i);
  if (ogDescMatch && ogDescMatch[1]) {
    return decodeHtmlEntities(ogDescMatch[1].trim());
  }

  // Try standard description meta tag
  const descMatch = html.match(/<meta[^>]*name=["']description["'][^>]*content=["']([^"']*)["'][^>]*\/?>/i)
    || html.match(/<meta[^>]*content=["']([^"']*)["'][^>]*name=["']description["'][^>]*\/?>/i);
  if (descMatch && descMatch[1]) {
    return decodeHtmlEntities(descMatch[1].trim());
  }

  // Try twitter:description meta tag
  const twitterDescMatch = html.match(/<meta[^>]*name=["']twitter:description["'][^>]*content=["']([^"']*)["'][^>]*\/?>/i)
    || html.match(/<meta[^>]*content=["']([^"']*)["'][^>]*name=["']twitter:description["'][^>]*\/?>/i);
  if (twitterDescMatch && twitterDescMatch[1]) {
    return decodeHtmlEntities(twitterDescMatch[1].trim());
  }

  return null;
}

/**
 * Decode common HTML entities.
 */
function decodeHtmlEntities(text: string): string {
  return text
    .replace(/&amp;/g, '&')
    .replace(/&lt;/g, '<')
    .replace(/&gt;/g, '>')
    .replace(/&quot;/g, '"')
    .replace(/&#39;/g, "'")
    .replace(/&#x27;/g, "'")
    .replace(/&#x2F;/g, '/')
    .replace(/&apos;/g, "'")
    .replace(/&#(\d+);/g, (_match, dec) => String.fromCharCode(Number(dec)))
    .replace(/&#x([0-9a-fA-F]+);/g, (_match, hex) => String.fromCharCode(parseInt(hex, 16)));
}

/**
 * Custom redirect validation: before following each redirect, validate the new destination.
 */
async function fetchWithValidatedRedirects(url: string, remainingRedirects: number): Promise<AxiosResponse> {
  // Validate the current URL
  await validateUrl(url);

  const response = await axios.get(url, {
    timeout: REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS,
    maxRedirects: 0, // Disable automatic redirects; we handle them manually
    validateStatus: (status: number) => status >= 200 && status < 400,
    headers: {
      'User-Agent': 'LinkPreviewBot/1.0',
      'Accept': 'text/html',
    },
    maxContentLength: 5 * 1024 * 1024, // 5MB max response size
    responseType: 'text',
  });

  // If it's a redirect response, validate the redirect destination
  if (response.status >= 300 && response.status < 400) {
    const redirectLocation: string | undefined = response.headers['location'];

    if (!redirectLocation) {
      throw new Error('Redirect response missing Location header');
    }

    if (remainingRedirects <= 0) {
      throw new Error(`Maximum number of redirects (${MAX_REDIRECTS}) exceeded`);
    }

    // Resolve relative redirect URLs against the current URL
    let absoluteRedirectUrl: string;
    try {
      const resolvedUrl = new URL(redirectLocation, url);
      absoluteRedirectUrl = resolvedUrl.toString();
    } catch {
      throw new Error('Invalid redirect URL');
    }

    // Recursively fetch with validation on the redirect target
    return fetchWithValidatedRedirects(absoluteRedirectUrl, remainingRedirects - 1);
  }

  return response;
}

/**
 * POST /api/link-preview
 * Accepts { url: string } in the request body and returns link preview metadata.
 */
app.post('/api/link-preview', async (req: Request, res: Response): Promise<void> => {
  try {
    const { url } = req.body as FetchRequest;

    // Validate input presence and type
    if (!url || typeof url !== 'string') {
      res.status(400).json({
        error: 'A valid URL string is required in the request body',
      });
      return;
    }

    // Trim and validate length
    const trimmedUrl: string = url.trim();
    if (trimmedUrl.length === 0 || trimmedUrl.length > 2048) {
      res.status(400).json({
        error: 'URL must be between 1 and 2048 characters',
      });
      return;
    }

    // Validate URL against allowlist and SSRF protections
    // This is done inside fetchWithValidatedRedirects as well,
    // but we do an initial check here for early error reporting.
    await validateUrl(trimmedUrl);

    // Fetch the URL content with validated redirect handling
    const response = await fetchWithValidatedRedirects(trimmedUrl, MAX_REDIRECTS);

    const html: string = typeof response.data === 'string' ? response.data : String(response.data);

    // Verify content type is HTML
    const contentType: string = response.headers['content-type'] || '';
    if (!contentType.includes('text/html') && !contentType.includes('application/xhtml+xml')) {
      res.status(400).json({
        error: 'The URL does not return HTML content',
      });
      return;
    }

    // Extract metadata
    const title = extractTitle(html);
    const description = extractDescription(html);

    const preview: LinkPreview = {
      url: trimmedUrl,
      title,
      description,
    };

    res.status(200).json(preview);
  } catch (error: unknown) {
    const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'An unexpected error occurred';

    // Distinguish between validation errors and fetch errors
    if (
      message.includes('not in the allowed domains') ||
      message.includes('private or reserved range') ||
      message.includes('Invalid URL') ||
      message.includes('Only HTTP and HTTPS') ||
      message.includes('authentication credentials') ||
      message.includes('Could not resolve hostname')
    ) {
      res.status(400).json({ error: message });
      return;
    }

    if (message.includes('Maximum number of redirects')) {
      res.status(400).json({ error: message });
      return;
    }

    // Log the full error server-side but return a generic message to the client
    console.error('Link preview fetch error:', error);
    res.status(502).json({
      error: 'Failed to fetch link preview',
    });
  }
});

/**
 * GET /api/health
 * Health check endpoint.
 */
app.get('/api/health', (_req: Request, res: Response): void => {
  res.status(200).json({ status: 'ok' });
});

const PORT = process.env.PORT || 3000;

app.listen(PORT, () => {
  console.log(`Link preview API server running on port ${PORT}`);
  console.log(`Allowed domains: ${Array.from(ALLOWED_DOMAINS).join(', ')}`);
});

export default app;
